questions.question: What is the primary evolutionary mechanism described by Charles Darwin where organisms better adapted to their environment tend to survive and produce more offspring?
What evolutionary phenomenon occurs when two unrelated species independently evolve similar traits as a result of having to adapt to similar environments?
What term describes the maximum population size of a biological species that a particular environment can sustain indefinitely given available resources?
Which ecological relationship describes a symbiosis where one species benefits while the other is completely unaffected (neither helped nor harmed)?
What is the random change in allele frequencies within a population over time, which exerts a much stronger effect in very small populations?
What are anatomical features in different organisms that share a common evolutionary origin and structural blueprint, regardless of their current function?
In an ecological energy pyramid, approximately what percentage of energy is successfully transferred from one trophic level up to the next highest level?
What type of speciation occurs when biological populations of the same species become isolated from each other due to a geographic barrier?
What is a species called that has a disproportionately large effect on its natural environment relative to its abundance, such that its removal collapses the ecosystem?
The Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium principle assumes several conditions to maintain constant allele frequencies. Which of the following violates that equilibrium?
